headline: AI’s Hottest Private Companies Have Booming Crypto Shadow Market
summary: (Bloomberg) -- The race to sell retail investors a piece of the AI boom has gone mainstream — closed-end funds, interval funds, special-purpose vehicles. Now, crypto platforms are offering trades tied to the most valuable private AI companies on earth — ones ordinary investors have almost no other way to access.Most Read from BloombergChina’s Unprecedented Defiance of US Sanctions Triggers ShowdownUS and Iran Trade Fire in Gulf, Jolting Four-Week-Old CeasefireFormer NYC Mayor Giuliani in Critica

headline: Robinhood CEO Defends the Math Behind 'Trump Accounts' Partnership
summary: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev addresses analyst pushback over the brokerage's government partnership to manage "Trump Accounts." Tenev explains why the accounts will rely heavily on human customer service rather than AI, and calls the initiative a "once-in-a-lifetime opportunity."

content.title: Robinhood CEO Defends the Math Behind 'Trump Accounts' Partnership
content.description: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev addresses analyst pushback over the brokerage's government partnership to manage "Trump Accounts."  Tenev explains why the accounts will rely heavily on human customer service rather than AI, and calls the initiative a "once-in-a-lifetime opportunity."

headline: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev’s Vision of the Future of Investing

headline: Market Minute 5-5-26- Palantir Profits, Coinbase Cuts, Yields in Focus
summary: After a moderate pullback on war worries yesterday, stocks are rising today. Gold and silver are rebounding as well, while crude oil is giving up ground. The dollar is flat, while Treasury yields continue to flirt with key levels.
